title: Let Loop Quantum Gravity and Affine Quantum Gravity Examine Each Other
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\2107.07879v1_Let Loop Quantum Gravity and Affine Quantum Gravity Examine .txt
summary: This paper compares Loop Quantum Gravity with Affine Quantum Gravity, two approaches to quantum gravity using different quantization methods. The authors analyze canonical and affine quantizations through simple model problems to illustrate key differences, then apply these insights to understand how each approach treats quantum gravity and Einstein's equations differently.

title: Loop Quantum Gravity Phenomenology: Linking Loops to Observational Physics
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1210.1485v2_Loop Quantum Gravity Phenomenology_ Linking Loops to Observa.txt
summary: This review examines phenomenological models connecting loop quantum gravity to observable physics at Planck scales. It discusses particle frameworks including modified dispersion relations, broken Poincaré symmetry, and generalized uncertainty principles, alongside field theory approaches with Lorentz violation and non-commutative geometry. The review analyzes arguments for both modified dispersion relations and Lorentz invariance preservation in LQG, reviews loop quantum cosmology phenomenology, and identifies open problems linking fundamental theory to testable predictions.

title: Group Field Theory and Loop Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1408.7112v1_Group Field Theory and Loop Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: This paper presents the group field theory (GFT) formalism as an approach to quantum gravity, emphasizing its connections to loop quantum gravity. It covers the foundational concepts of GFT, reviews recent developments in the field, and discusses promising future research directions within this framework.

title: Philosophical Foundations of Loop Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\2211.06718v2_Philosophical Foundations of Loop Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: This paper reviews the conceptual and philosophical foundations of Loop Quantum Gravity, addressing how space and time emerge at the quantum level, the nature of locality in quantum gravity, computational methods using finite graphs, the problem of time, and how observable quantities are characterized in a quantum gravitational framework.

title: The spin-foam-representation of loop quantum gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\0601095v1_The spin-foam-representation of loop quantum gravity.txt
summary: This paper presents the spin-foam representation as a systematic approach to defining dynamics in loop quantum gravity through a path integral formulation. The authors develop this framework rigorously in 2+1 dimensions via canonical quantization, clarifying the geometric interpretation of histories in generally covariant theories. They extend similar structures to four dimensions as spin-foam models, while highlighting open issues regarding canonical connections and renormalizability, and discuss implications for quantum spacetime arising from background independence.

title: Loop Quantum Gravity and Quantum Information
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\2302.05922v1_Loop Quantum Gravity and Quantum Information.txt
summary: This paper explores the intersection of loop quantum gravity and quantum information theory, focusing on how entanglement structures encode quantum geometry. It examines link, intertwiner, and boundary spin entanglement in spin-network states, demonstrating how entanglement patterns reconstruct spacetime quanta. The work identifies area-law entanglement entropy as characteristic of semiclassical geometries, using information-theoretic bounds to probe the emergence of classical geometry from quantum states.

title: Towards a Covariant Loop Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\0608135v1_Towards a Covariant Loop Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: This paper presents a covariant reformulation of Loop Quantum Gravity by performing canonical analysis of the Palatini action while preserving Lorentz gauge symmetry, avoiding the standard time gauge fixing. The resulting theory eliminates the Immirzi ambiguity, yields a continuous area spectrum, employs Lorentz group spin networks, and naturally connects to Barrett-Crane spin foam models.

title: New Spin Foam Models of Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\0501091v1_New Spin Foam Models of Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: This paper reviews the Barrett-Crane spin foam models and introduces two new spin foam approaches to quantum gravity. The first derives from embedded spin networks in loop quantum gravity, while the second emerges from path integral quantization of the Palatini action with tetrad integration. Both models address limitations of existing Barrett-Crane formulations through direct quantization of General Relativity.

title: Canonical ``Loop'' Quantum Gravity and Spin Foam Models
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\9903076v2_Canonical __Loop__ Quantum Gravity and Spin Foam Models.txt
summary: This paper reviews canonical loop quantum gravity and its connection to spin foam models. It discusses key achievements including discrete spectra of geometric observables and black hole entropy derivation, then demonstrates how the Wheeler-DeWitt constraint leads to spin foam formulations in 4D. The authors argue spin foams provide a natural framework unifying loop quantum gravity with topological field theories like BF theory.

title: Space and Time in Loop Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1802.02382v1_Space and Time in Loop Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: This paper examines how Loop Quantum Gravity modifies fundamental concepts of space and time at the quantum level. The author discusses and clarifies the specific mechanisms through which LQG requires reconceptualizations of classical spacetime, addressing a central question in quantum gravity research about the nature of geometry at Planck scales.

title: Discretisations, Constraints and Diffeomorphisms in Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1111.1879v2_Discretisations_ Constraints and Diffeomorphisms in Quantum .txt
summary: This review examines how discretization, constraint implementation, and diffeomorphism symmetry interact in Loop Quantum Gravity and Spin Foam models. It presents the Consistent Discretizations approach, applying the master constraint program to construct physical Hilbert spaces, and the Perfect Actions approach for finding path integral measures respecting diffeomorphism symmetry.

title: BTZ Black Hole Entropy in Loop Quantum Gravity and in Spin Foam Models
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1303.2773v3_BTZ Black Hole Entropy in Loop Quantum Gravity and in Spin F.txt
summary: This paper compares entropy calculations for BTZ black holes using two quantum gravity approaches: loop quantum gravity and spin foam models. The authors demonstrate that both formalisms yield identical results for BTZ black hole entropy, providing consistency evidence between these different quantization schemes for three-dimensional gravity.

title: Spin-Foams for All Loop Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\0909.0939v5_Spin-Foams for All Loop Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: This paper generalizes the EPRL spin-foam models from simplicial complexes to arbitrary linear 2-cell spin-foams, enabling them to accommodate all spin-network states of loop quantum gravity without restriction to triangulations. The framework is extended to handle arbitrary vertex valencies and allows knotted/linked spin-foam histories. A key result establishes a one-to-one correspondence between SU(2) and SU(2)×SU(2) EPRL intertwiners for Barbero-Immirzi parameter |γ|≥1.

title: Dynamics of loop quantum gravity and spin foam models in three dimensions
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\0402112v2_Dynamics of loop quantum gravity and spin foam models in thr.txt
summary: This paper rigorously regularizes Rovelli's generalized projection operator in canonical 2+1 gravity, establishing an explicit connection between loop quantum gravity and spin foam models in three dimensions. The results provide a foundational framework that offers new insights for addressing dynamical problems in 3+1 dimensional quantum gravity.

title: Topspin Networks in Loop Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1111.1252v2_Topspin Networks in Loop Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: This paper extends loop quantum gravity by incorporating topological information into spin networks through the topspin network formalism. The authors demonstrate that encoding topology requires only minimal modifications to the phase space, C*-algebra, and Hilbert space of cylindrical functions. They analyze how the area and Hamiltonian operators depend on topological structure, thereby extending background independence in LQG to encompass both geometric and topological degrees of freedom.

title: From Classical To Quantum Gravity: Introduction to Loop Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1203.2733v2_From Classical To Quantum Gravity_ Introduction to Loop Quan.txt
summary: This paper provides an introduction to loop quantum gravity's canonical quantization approach, emphasizing new matter coupling proposals for deparametrization and improved dynamics. It covers both classical and quantum aspects of these new formulations alongside standard LQG quantization of vacuum general relativity.

title: The ADM papers and part of their modern legacy: loop quantum gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1505.02089v1_The ADM papers and part of their modern legacy_ loop quantum.txt
summary: This paper provides an accessible overview of loop quantum gravity and its historical connections to the foundational ADM formalism from the 1960s. It traces how the Arnowitt-Deser-Misner framework contributed to the development of modern loop quantum gravity, explaining key concepts for non-specialist audiences.

title: Loop Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1412.4362v1_Loop Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: This review presents loop quantum gravity (LQG), a background-independent nonperturbative approach to quantum gravity. It covers the canonical formulation of LQG as the primary focus, discusses fundamental ideas and their significance, and briefly addresses spin foam theory, black hole thermodynamics, and loop quantum cosmology, while summarizing current directions and open problems in the field.

title: Comparison of area spectra in loop quantum gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\0401110v3_Comparison of area spectra in loop quantum gravity.txt
summary: We compare two area spectra proposed in loop quantum gravity in different approaches to compute the entropy of the Schwarzschild black hole. We describe the black hole in general microcanonical and ca

title: Introduction to Loop Quantum Gravity and Spin Foams
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\0409061v3_Introduction to Loop Quantum Gravity and Spin Foams.txt
summary: Didactic overview of loop quantum gravity and spin foams, covering the non-perturbative, background-independent quantization of gravity using connection variables. Discusses constraint quantization, Planck-scale discreteness of geometry, black hole entropy computation, and extends to spin foam formalism for both 2+1 and 4D gravity.

title: Loop quantum gravity - a short review
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1001.4188v3_Loop quantum gravity - a short review.txt
summary: This review article presents the foundations and current status of loop quantum gravity, a non-perturbative approach to quantum gravity. It covers kinematical quantization, Hamilton constraint implementation, quantum black hole horizon theory, semiclassical states, and matter propagation, while deferring detailed discussion of spin foam models and loop quantum cosmology to separate reviews.

title: Coarse graining spin foam quantum gravity -- a review
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\2007.01315v1_Coarse graining spin foam quantum gravity -- a review.txt
summary: This review examines renormalization and coarse graining in spin foam quantum gravity, a discrete approach to quantum gravity. It discusses how to derive effective theories at coarser scales from microscopic dynamics, emphasizing boundary data's crucial role in background-independent renormalization. The review covers motivations for renormalization including restoration of diffeomorphism symmetry, concrete coarse graining examples, tensor network methods, recent developments, and future research directions.

title: Loop quantum gravity of a spherically symmetric scalar field coupled to gravity with a clock
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\2303.09392v1_Loop quantum gravity of a spherically symmetric scalar field.txt
summary: This paper addresses the challenge of incorporating matter fields into spherically symmetric loop quantum gravity by introducing a scalar clock field. Using the Abelianization technique, the authors gauge-fix the system to obtain a true Hamiltonian with simplified dynamics, avoiding constraint algebra complications. They demonstrate that loop quantum gravity methods applied to this framework recover known results for Schwarzschild black hole states and observables.

title: Conformal loop quantum gravity coupled to the Standard Model
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1609.04028v1_Conformal loop quantum gravity coupled to the Standard Model.txt
summary: This paper proposes a conformally invariant extension of general relativity coupled to the Standard Model as the fundamental theory to be quantized using loop quantum gravity techniques. Through gauge fixing and a modified Higgs mechanism, the theory recovers conventional general relativity with massive particles. The framework provides new perspectives on the Hamiltonian constraint in LQG, semiclassical limits, renormalization in quantum spacetime, and backreaction calculations.
approaches: Quantum Field Theory on Curved Spacetime

title: On Loop States in Loop Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\0611156v2_On Loop States in Loop Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: This paper constructs and characterizes all independent loop states in 3+1 dimensional loop quantum gravity using a lattice regularization in the Hamiltonian formalism. Loop states are characterized by angular momentum quantum numbers representing SU(2) rigid rotators on lattice links. The authors use Schwinger bosons to construct these states and apply the generalized Wigner-Eckart theorem to compute matrix elements of the volume operator, explicitly constructing simple volume eigenstates.

title: Loop Quantum Gravity and the Meaning of Diffeomorphism Invariance
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\9910079v2_Loop Quantum Gravity and the Meaning of Diffeomorphism Invar.txt
summary: This paper provides a comprehensive introduction to loop quantum gravity, focusing on the non-perturbative, background-independent canonical quantization of gravity. It constructs the kinematical Hilbert space using spin network states, analyzes the area operator to demonstrate geometric discreteness, and discusses diffeomorphism invariance's role in defining observables. The work also outlines covariant formulations through spin foam models.

title: Spin Foam Models of Riemannian Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\0202017v4_Spin Foam Models of Riemannian Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: This paper numerically compares three versions of the Barrett-Crane spin foam model for 4D Riemannian quantum gravity. The original model with standard amplitudes shows rapid partition function divergence, while the Perez-Rovelli modified version exhibits excessive spin-zero dominance. A new improved version is proposed with convergent partition function behavior, addressing fundamental issues in extracting physical predictions from spin foam models.

title: Three dimensional loop quantum gravity: physical scalar product and spin foam models
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\0402110v3_Three dimensional loop quantum gravity_ physical scalar prod.txt
summary: This paper constructs a rigorous definition of the physical scalar product in three-dimensional loop quantum gravity by establishing a connection between canonical quantization and spin-foam models. The authors define a generalized projection operator from the kinematical to physical Hilbert space without introducing cutoffs or encountering divergences in spin sums, providing a mathematically clean link between these two quantization approaches.

title: Effective Spin Foam Models for Lorentzian Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\2104.00485v2_Effective Spin Foam Models for Lorentzian Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: This paper develops effective spin foam models for Lorentzian quantum gravity, defining a path integral over discrete quantum geometric configurations including metric and torsion degrees of freedom. The torsion emerges from an anomaly parametrized by the Barbero-Immirzi parameter. The computationally efficient effective models enable semi-classical regime tests and exploration of Lorentzian-specific phenomena such as null geometric structures and spatial topology change.

title: Gauge-invariant bounce from loop quantum gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1906.02759v2_Gauge-invariant bounce from loop quantum gravity.txt
summary: This paper develops a gauge-invariant formulation of loop quantum gravity to resolve cosmological singularities. The analysis shows that quantum geometric effects replace the big bang with an asymmetric bounce when spacetime curvature reaches Planck scale. Notably, effective constants like Newton's constant rescale across the bounce, and minimally coupled matter becomes effectively non-minimally coupled near the bounce, potentially generating observable signatures.

title: Loop Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\gr-qc_9710008_Loop Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: Loop quantum gravity is a non-perturbative, background-independent quantization of general relativity that provides a mathematically well-defined approach to quantum gravity. Key achievements include computing discrete spectra of geometrical quantities like area and volume at Planck scale, deriving black hole entropy, and revealing quantum spacetime's polymer-like microstructure consistent with Wheeler's spacetime foam picture. Recent progress has resolved fundamental issues including scalar product definition and reality conditions, though dynamics formulation remains open.

title: Quantum Spacetime Phenomenology
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\0806.0339_Quantum Spacetime Phenomenology.txt
summary: This review examines phenomenological approaches to quantum spacetime, highlighting experimental methods sensitive to Planck-scale effects and emphasizing how phenomenological programs have influenced quantum gravity theory development. It focuses on observational tests and data analysis techniques that probe fundamental spacetime structure.

title: Chern-Simons Modified General Relativity

title: Quantum Gravity and Extra Dimensions at High-Energy Colliders
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-ph_9811291_Quantum Gravity and Extra Dimensions at High-Energy Collider.txt
summary: This paper investigates collider signatures of quantum gravity in theories with large extra dimensions where the fundamental gravity scale could be as low as the TeV scale. The authors construct an effective field theory of Kaluza-Klein graviton excitations and analyze their production and exchange at high-energy colliders, including the LHC, Tevatron, and e+e- colliders, finding that these experiments can probe fundamental gravity scales up to several TeV depending on the number of extra dimensions.

title: Black holes as mirrors: quantum information in random subsystems
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\0708.4025_Black holes as mirrors_ quantum information in random subsys.txt
summary: This paper analyzes quantum information retrieval from evaporating black holes by modeling internal black hole dynamics as unitary, rapidly mixing processes. The authors demonstrate that information deposited after the halfway point (when half the initial entropy has radiated away) emerges quickly in Hawking radiation, while information deposited earlier remains hidden until that threshold. The analysis leverages quantum error-correcting codes and quantum erasure channels, finding results marginally consistent with black hole complementarity.

title: The String Landscape and the Swampland
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_0509212_The String Landscape and the Swampland.txt
summary: This paper introduces the concept of the 'swampland' - the vast space of effective field theories that appear consistent but are actually incompatible with quantum gravity. Using universality arguments from string theory, the authors propose that the string theory landscape occupies only a small corner of all possible consistent-looking effective theories, and establish finiteness criteria to identify the boundary between valid quantum gravitational theories and the swampland.

title: Building up spacetime with quantum entanglement
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1005.3035_Building up spacetime with quantum entanglement.txt
summary: This paper argues that the classical structure of spacetime emerges from quantum entanglement in non-perturbative quantum gravity. The authors demonstrate that disentangling degrees of freedom associated with different spacetime regions causes those regions to become disconnected, with the degree of separation quantifiable through entanglement measures.

title: Generalized gravitational entropy
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1304.4926_Generalized gravitational entropy.txt
summary: This paper generalizes the black hole entropy formula to Euclidean gravity solutions with boundaries containing non-contractible circles. The authors argue that entropy of the associated density matrix in quantum gravity is given by minimal surface area, without requiring a Killing vector. This theoretical framework explains the Ryu-Takayanagi conjecture for entanglement entropy in gauge/gravity duality.

title: Nonperturbative Evolution Equation for Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_9605030_Nonperturbative Evolution Equation for Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: This paper introduces a scale-dependent effective action for quantum gravity and derives an exact nonperturbative evolution equation governing its renormalization group flow. The approach maintains general coordinate invariance and modified BRS symmetry. Solutions in a truncated action space reveal nonperturbative corrections to Newton's constant's beta-function in 2+ε dimensions and demonstrate antiscreening behavior in 4D Einstein gravity, where Newton's constant increases at large distances.

title: Evanescent Black Holes
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_9111056_Evanescent Black Holes.txt
summary: This paper analyzes a renormalizable two-dimensional quantum gravity theory coupled to a dilaton and conformal matter. The authors show the theory is classically exact-solvable and study black hole formation via matter collapse. Using 1/N expansion, they demonstrate that Hawking radiation causes collapsing matter to radiate away all energy before forming an event horizon, suggesting black holes vanish from the quantum spectrum. The results indicate matter asymptotically forms zero-energy bound states carrying quantum numbers, with a unitary S-matrix potentially accommodating such states.

title: Black Hole Entropy from Near-Horizon Microstates
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_9712251_Black Hole Entropy from Near-Horizon Microstates.txt
summary: This paper computes black hole entropy microscopically for black holes with near-horizon AdS₃ geometry by leveraging the conformal field theory description of quantum gravity on AdS₃. The authors demonstrate precise agreement with the Bekenstein-Hawking formula without relying on string theory or supersymmetry, suggesting the result applies broadly to consistent quantum gravity theories.

title: Information in Black Hole Radiation
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_9306083_Information in Black Hole Radiation.txt
summary: This paper examines whether information lost in black hole formation can be recovered in the emitted radiation through S-matrix analysis. The authors estimate that information recovery is highly suppressed—either emerging extremely slowly or spread so diffusely that it remains invisible to perturbative expansions in M_Planck/M or 1/N for large-N dilatonic black holes, challenging standard perturbative approaches to the black hole information problem.

title: The Stretched Horizon and Black Hole Complementarity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_9306069_The Stretched Horizon and Black Hole Complementarity.txt
summary: This paper develops a stretched horizon framework for black holes based on three postulates of quantum mechanics, semi-classical gravity, and thermodynamics. Using dilaton gravity in 1+1 dimensions, the authors explain how dissipative horizon properties emerge from coarse-graining microscopic degrees of freedom, introducing black hole complementarity as a key principle for reconciling information conservation with horizon physics observed by distant observers.

title: The Swampland: Introduction and Review
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1903.06239_The Swampland_ Introduction and Review.txt
summary: The Swampland program provides a framework to identify which effective field theories are consistent with quantum gravity versus those excluded by quantum gravity constraints. This introduction and review covers key conjectures including the Weak Gravity Conjecture, string theory compactifications, and the de Sitter conjecture, establishing criteria that any valid UV-complete theory must satisfy.

title: Loop Quantum Cosmology: A Status Report
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1108.0893_Loop Quantum Cosmology_ A Status Report.txt
summary: This paper provides a comprehensive status report on loop quantum cosmology (LQC), reviewing recent developments and applications for multiple audiences. It covers foundational concepts, technical advances, and cosmological implications of LQC, including modifications to early universe physics. The article is structured to be accessible to researchers entering the field, the quantum gravity community, and cosmologists interested in alternative early universe scenarios.

title: The String Dilaton and a Least Coupling Principle
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_9401069_The String Dilaton and a Least Coupling Principle.txt
summary: This paper proposes a mechanism for fixing the vacuum expectation value of a massless dilaton through string-loop corrections to matter couplings. A 'Least Coupling Principle' drives the dilaton toward decoupling from matter during cosmological evolution, resulting in a weakly coupled state compatible with experiments. The work predicts small deviations from general relativity and motivates improved tests of the Equivalence Principle.

title: Black Holes at the LHC
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-ph_0106295_Black Holes at the LHC.txt
summary: This paper proposes that if quantum gravity operates at TeV scales, the LHC can produce black holes at high rates. Black holes decay into photons and leptons, providing clean experimental signatures. The mass and temperature measurements of these decaying black holes enable tests of Hawking evaporation laws in higher dimensions, potentially revealing the number of extra dimensions and the quantum gravity scale.

title: De Sitter Space and the Swampland
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1806.08362_De Sitter Space and the Swampland.txt
summary: This paper proposes a swampland criterion stating that the gradient of any scalar potential in quantum gravity must satisfy |∇V| ≥ c·V, which forbids metastable de Sitter vacua. The authors argue this constraint is universal across consistent quantum gravity theories, motivated by string theory no-go theorems and constructions, and extend these results to constrain accelerating universe scenarios.

title: Perturbative Quantum Gravity as a Double Copy of Gauge Theory
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1004.0476_Perturbative Quantum Gravity as a Double Copy of Gauge Theor.txt
summary: This paper establishes that perturbative quantum gravity amplitudes can be derived as double copies of gauge theory amplitudes by exploiting a color-kinematics duality. The authors conjecture this duality extends to all loop orders and demonstrate it explicitly at three loops for N=4 super-Yang-Mills theory, recovering N=8 supergravity amplitudes. This reveals a deep structural relationship between gauge theory and gravity at the quantum level.

title: Testing the nature of dark compact objects: a status report
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1904.05363_Testing the nature of dark compact objects_ a status report.txt
summary: This paper reviews the observational status and physics of dark compact objects as probes of extreme gravitational fields. It examines exotic alternatives to black holes and their implications for fundamental physics puzzles including dark matter, singularities, and unitarity. The paper synthesizes gravitational wave astronomy and very long baseline interferometry observations to test the Kerr black hole paradigm and constrain beyond-standard-model physics.

title: A Generalized Uncertainty Principle in Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_9301067_A Generalized Uncertainty Principle in Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: This paper derives a generalized uncertainty principle (GUP) through a thought experiment measuring black hole apparent horizon area in quantum gravity. The analysis reveals a fundamental minimum length scale on the order of the Planck length, independent of the specific quantum gravity framework. The results suggest black holes below the Planck mass lack operational definition and align with predictions from string theory.

title: Quantum Geometry and Black Hole Entropy
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\gr-qc_9710007_Quantum Geometry and Black Hole Entropy.txt
summary: This paper establishes a fundamental connection between quantum geometry and black hole thermodynamics by showing that black hole entropy arises from Chern-Simons theory on the horizon in loop quantum gravity. The entropy is derived to be proportional to horizon area with a proportionality constant determined by the Immirzi parameter, recovering the Bekenstein-Hawking formula for non-rotating and charged black holes without requiring near-extremal conditions.

title: 2D Gravity and Random Matrices
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_9306153_2D Gravity and Random Matrices.txt
summary: This review examines 2D gravity coupled to conformal matter with central charge d<1 using random matrix theory. It presents exact solutions via orthogonal polynomials and differential operators, discusses saddle-point approximations, and addresses non-Borel summability issues in perturbation theory. The paper connects matrix models to topological gravity, Painlevé equations, and moduli space intersection theory, providing a comprehensive framework for understanding discrete gravity through matrix formulations.

title: Symmetries and Strings in Field Theory and Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1011.5120_Symmetries and Strings in Field Theory and Gravity.txt
summary: This paper analyzes global and gauged symmetries in quantum field theory and quantum gravity, emphasizing discrete gauge symmetries and their connection to emergent one-form Kalb-Ramond symmetries. The authors uncover new observables in nonlinear σ-models, expand Polchinski's cosmic string classification, and argue that quantum gravity forbids global symmetries while requiring compact gauge symmetries and Dirac-quantized charges. These insights provide a unified perspective on supergravity consistency conditions.

title: Flux Compactification
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_0610102_Flux Compactification.txt

title: Brane Inflation
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-ph_9812483_Brane Inflation.txt
summary: This paper proposes a brane inflation mechanism in theories with TeV-scale quantum gravity and large extra dimensions. Standard model particles are confined to branes while gravity propagates in the bulk. Inflation is triggered by vacuum energy from displaced branes in extra dimensions, described in 4D effective theory as a slowly-rolling scalar field with flat plateau potential. The scenario naturally produces large e-foldings and ends when branes approach critical distances, followed by brane collision and oscillation.

title: Modern tests of Lorentz invariance
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\gr-qc_0502097_Modern tests of Lorentz invariance.txt
summary: This review examines theoretical frameworks and experimental tests of Lorentz invariance motivated by quantum gravity. It synthesizes terrestrial and astrophysical constraints on Lorentz violation, presenting current precision limits and methodologies for detecting potential violations across multiple energy regimes.

title: Nonstandard optics from quantum spacetime
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\gr-qc_9809038_Nonstandard optics from quantum spacetime.txt
summary: This paper investigates light propagation through semiclassical spacetime emerging from loop quantum gravity, where spacetime exhibits a discrete polymer-like structure. The authors compute modifications to Maxwell's equations from quantum gravity effects, finding helicity-dependent corrections to wave propagation. These predictions could be tested using gamma-ray burst observations to constrain quantum spacetime properties and test discrete nature of spacetime.

title: Random Matrix Theories in Quantum Physics: Common Concepts
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\cond-mat_9707301_Random Matrix Theories in Quantum Physics_ Common Concepts.txt
summary: This review covers random matrix theory (RMT) development over a decade, emphasizing theoretical foundations and applications across chaotic systems, disorder, localization, many-body quantum systems, the Calogero-Sutherland model, QCD chiral symmetry breaking, and 2D quantum gravity. The paper highlights universal concepts underlying diverse RMT applications and proposes that universality principles based on symmetry and stochasticity constitute a new statistical mechanics framework independent of microscopic dynamics.

title: Superspace, or One thousand and one lessons in supersymmetry
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_0108200_Superspace_ or One thousand and one lessons in supersymmetry.txt
summary: A comprehensive pedagogical text on supersymmetry and superspace formalism, originally published in 1983 and now made freely available with corrections and improved typesetting. The work covers fundamental concepts and applications of supersymmetric theories across a thousand lessons.

title: Holography, Thermodynamics and Fluctuations of Charged AdS Black Holes
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_9904197_Holography_ Thermodynamics and Fluctuations of Charged AdS B.txt
summary: This paper explores the thermodynamic properties of charged AdS black holes using holography, revealing a van der Waals-Maxwell phase structure analogous to liquid-gas systems. The authors analyze fluctuations, stability, and critical phenomena in both canonical and grand canonical ensembles, discovering a second-order phase transition near the critical point characterized by a Landau-Ginzburg A₃ potential. The holographic dual describes microscopic degrees of freedom underlying the thermodynamics.

title: The Kerr/CFT Correspondence
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\0809.4266_The Kerr_CFT Correspondence.txt
summary: This paper establishes the Kerr/CFT correspondence, showing that quantum gravity near the horizon of an extreme Kerr black hole is dual to a two-dimensional conformal field theory. The authors derive Virasoro algebra symmetries with specific central charge, demonstrate that black hole microstates correspond to CFT states, and verify that the Cardy formula reproduces the Bekenstein-Hawking entropy, providing a microscopic accounting of extreme Kerr black hole entropy.

title: Quantum Gravity In De Sitter Space
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_0106109_Quantum Gravity In De Sitter Space.txt
summary: This paper examines quantum gravity in de Sitter space, arguing that the finite-dimensional Hilbert space suggests General Relativity cannot be quantized independently but must be embedded in a theory determining the cosmological constant. The authors define a quantum Hilbert space using asymptotic boundary conditions and discuss fundamental difficulties in defining measurable observables in asymptotically de Sitter spacetime, introducing meta-observables with mathematical meaning but physical unmeasurability.

title: Generalized Uncertainty Principle in Quantum Gravity from Micro-Black Hole Gedanken Experiment
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_9904025_Generalized Uncertainty Principle in Quantum Gravity from Mi.txt
summary: This paper derives a Generalized Uncertainty Principle (GUP) through a gedanken experiment involving micro-black holes at the Planck scale. The approach combines the Heisenberg uncertainty principle with the Schwarzschild radius formula, showing that a GUP emerges model-independently without requiring a specific quantum gravity framework. The work connects string theory predictions with fundamental black hole physics.

title: On BMS Invariance of Gravitational Scattering
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1312.2229_On BMS Invariance of Gravitational Scattering.txt
summary: This paper establishes BMS invariance of gravitational scattering by identifying diagonal BMS0 symmetries that connect future and past null infinity using Christodoulou-Klainerman results. BMS0 is shown to be an infinite-dimensional symmetry of the classical gravitational S-matrix and quantum gravity, implying energy flux conservation at every angle on the conformal sphere. The associated Ward identity relates S-matrix elements with different soft graviton content, and BMS0 is recast as a U(1) Kac-Moody symmetry with an expression for the current in terms of soft graviton operators.

title: On Geometric Entropy
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_9401072_On Geometric Entropy.txt
summary: This paper demonstrates that a geometrical entropy concept in flat spacetime determines the leading quantum correction to Bekenstein-Hawking black hole entropy. The authors present two calculation methods: a direct Hamiltonian approach and a Euclidean heat kernel method. The entropy exhibits divergences in quantum field theory without UV regularization, though finite quantities can be extracted. The work extends considerations to string theory.
keywords: quantum field theory in curved spacetime

title: Black Hole Entropy in Canonical Quantum Gravity and Superstring Theory
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_9401070_Black Hole Entropy in Canonical Quantum Gravity and Superstr.txt
summary: This paper examines black hole entropy in both canonical quantum gravity and superstring theory for infinite-mass Schwarzschild black holes. It demonstrates that entropy per unit area from quantum fields diverges quadratically near the horizon, relating these divergences to quantum corrections of gravitational coupling. Superstring theory provides finite entropy through open strings attached to the horizon, offering insights into black hole information loss.
approaches: Quantum Field Theory in Curved Spacetime

title: A Spin Entanglement Witness for Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1707.06050_A Spin Entanglement Witness for Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: This paper proposes a testable method to verify gravity's quantum nature by demonstrating gravitational entanglement between two micron-sized test masses in separate matter-wave interferometers. The key innovation is a spin entanglement witness protocol based on correlation measurements between spins embedded in each mass. The authors show that despite gravity's weakness, gravitational interaction can create detectable entanglement far enough to exclude Casimir-Polder forces, providing direct experimental evidence that gravity acts as a quantum coherent mediator.
approaches: quantum field theory in curved spacetime

title: Quantum Nature of the Big Bang
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\gr-qc_0602086_Quantum Nature of the Big Bang.txt
summary: This paper resolves the quantum nature of the big bang singularity in homogeneous isotropic models with a scalar field using loop quantum cosmology. The scalar field serves as an internal clock realizing emergent time, and the physical Hilbert space is rigorously constructed. Numerical solutions of the Hamiltonian constraint demonstrate that the big bang singularity is replaced by a quantum bounce, with deterministic evolution across the Planck regime.

title: Real Ashtekar Variables for Lorentzian Signature Space-times
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\gr-qc_9410014_Real Ashtekar Variables for Lorentzian Signature Space-times.txt
summary: This letter proposes a new strategy for handling reality conditions in the Ashtekar formalism by reformulating the Hamiltonian constraint in SO(3) Yang-Mills phase space. The approach enables description of Lorentzian signature spacetimes using real variables instead of complex ones, while preserving the geometric properties of Ashtekar variables and maintaining compatibility with loop quantum gravity quantization methods.

title: BMS supertranslations and Weinberg's soft graviton theorem
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1401.7026_BMS supertranslations and Weinberg_s soft graviton theorem.txt
summary: This paper establishes the equivalence between BMS supertranslation Ward identities and Weinberg's soft graviton theorem in quantum gravity. The authors construct canonical generators of supertranslations at null infinity, incorporating soft graviton contributions as boundary modes. The work demonstrates that soft gravitons are Goldstone bosons of spontaneously broken supertranslation symmetry, connecting asymptotic symmetries to fundamental scattering amplitude properties.

title: Minimal Length Scale Scenarios for Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1203.6191_Minimal Length Scale Scenarios for Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: This review examines whether fundamental physics limits probing arbitrarily short distances through minimal length scale scenarios. It synthesizes insights from thought experiments and various quantum gravity approaches, discusses implementation models like the generalized uncertainty principle and modified dispersion relations, and explores their effects across quantum mechanics, QED, thermodynamics, black holes, and cosmology.

title: Gravitationally-induced entanglement between two massive particles is sufficient evidence of quantum effects in gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1707.06036_Gravitationally-induced entanglement between two massive par.txt
summary: This paper proposes a quantum-information-theoretic approach to test quantum gravity by detecting gravitationally-induced entanglement between two massive particles in spatial superpositions. The authors prove that any mediating field capable of generating entanglement between quantum systems must itself be quantum, and argue that observing such entanglement would provide evidence of gravity's quantization without requiring direct quantum control over gravity itself.
approaches: quantum field theory in curved spacetime

title: Palatini Approach to Modified Gravity: f(R) Theories and Beyond
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1101.3864_Palatini Approach to Modified Gravity_ f_R_ Theories and Bey.txt
summary: This review examines modified gravity theories using the Palatini formalism, where metric and connection are treated as independent variables. It covers f(R) theories and extensions, analyzing cosmic acceleration, observational tests, stellar structure, mathematical well-posedness, and bouncing cosmologies while discussing connections to dark matter, dark energy, and quantum gravity phenomenology.

title: Quantum Theory of Gravity I: Area Operators
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\gr-qc_9602046_Quantum Theory of Gravity I_ Area Operators.txt
summary: This paper develops a non-perturbative quantum theory of geometry by constructing regulated area operators on a kinematical Hilbert space. The operators are shown to be self-adjoint with purely discrete spectra, revealing that quantum geometry is fundamentally discrete with polymer-like excitations rather than continuous. The Hilbert space decomposes into finite-dimensional spin system subspaces, enabling complete evaluation of area operator spectra and providing a framework for studying higher-dimensional geometric operators.

title: Spectral Dimension of the Universe in Quantum Gravity at a Lifshitz Point
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\0902.3657_Spectral Dimension of the Universe in Quantum Gravity at a L.txt
summary: This paper extends spectral dimension definitions to quantum gravity theories with anisotropic scaling at Lifshitz points. The authors derive that spectral dimension equals 1+D/z for a theory with dynamical critical exponent z in D+1 dimensions. For 3+1 gravity with z flowing from 3 (UV) to 1 (IR), spectral dimension flows from 4 to 2, matching numerical results from causal dynamical triangulations.

title: Quantum Gravity in Everyday Life: General Relativity as an Effective Field Theory
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\gr-qc_0311082_Quantum Gravity in Everyday Life_ General Relativity as an E.txt
summary: This paper provides a comprehensive review of effective field theory methods applied to gravity, treating general relativity as an effective field theory valid at low energies. It covers the theoretical framework, quantum calculations, and applications of EFT techniques to gravitational systems, offering an accessible introduction to how quantum corrections to gravity can be systematically computed without requiring a complete theory of quantum gravity.

title: Models of AdS_2 Backreaction and Holography
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1402.6334_Models of AdS_2 Backreaction and Holography.txt
summary: This paper constructs one-dimensional dilaton gravity models that flow to AdS₂ from higher-dimensional AdS spaces and explores how backreaction effects modify holographic correlators. The authors demonstrate that backreaction scales as a relevant effect at low energies in compact transverse geometries and examines matter loop contributions similar to the CGHS model framework.

title: Isolated and dynamical horizons and their applications
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\gr-qc_0407042_Isolated and dynamical horizons and their applications.txt
summary: This review presents a quasi-local framework for analyzing black holes through isolated horizons (equilibrium) and dynamical horizons (evolving). The framework unifies multiple approaches to black hole physics across quantum gravity, mathematical physics, and numerical relativity, providing new insights into black hole thermodynamics, entropy calculations, hairy black holes, numerical simulations, and dynamical laws in general relativity.

title: Planck-Scale Physics and the Peccei-Quinn Mechanism
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_9202003_Planck-Scale Physics and the Peccei-Quinn Mechanism.txt
summary: This paper examines how Planck-scale physics effects, manifested through global-symmetry-violating higher-dimensional operators, impact the Peccei-Quinn mechanism for solving the strong-CP problem. The authors demonstrate that such operators substantially modify axion properties and undermine the naturalness of the Peccei-Quinn solution, with implications extending to other global symmetries in particle physics and cosmology.

title: Causality Constraints on Corrections to the Graviton Three-Point Coupling
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1407.5597_Causality Constraints on Corrections to the Graviton Three-P.txt
summary: This paper establishes causality constraints on higher derivative corrections to the graviton three-point coupling in weakly coupled gravity theories. Using a high-energy scattering thought experiment, the authors demonstrate that additional Lorentz-invariant structures beyond Einstein gravity lead to causality violations that cannot be remedied by conventional particles but require an infinite tower of massive higher-spin particles. In AdS/CFT, this constrains conformal anomaly coefficients; in cosmology, it predicts massive higher-spin particles if primordial gravitational wave non-gaussianity deviates from general relativity predictions.

title: Universality of Quantum Gravity Corrections
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\0810.5333_Universality of Quantum Gravity Corrections.txt
summary: This paper demonstrates that quantum gravity corrections from a minimum measurable length and the Generalized Uncertainty Principle (GUP) universally affect all quantum systems. The authors calculate specific corrections to the Lamb Shift, Landau levels, and STM tunneling currents, showing these predictions either represent testable quantum gravity effects or provide experimental constraints on GUP parameters at electroweak scales.

title: Science with the Cherenkov Telescope Array
authors: E. M. de Gouveia Dal Pino
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1709.07997_Science with the Cherenkov Telescope Array.txt
summary: The Cherenkov Telescope Array (CTA) is a next-generation gamma-ray observatory covering 20 GeV to 300 TeV with 99 southern and 19 northern telescopes. CTA will conduct high-energy astrophysics research including dark matter searches, cosmic particle acceleration, black hole environments, gravitational wave counterparts, and transient phenomena through coordinated multi-messenger observations with global astronomical networks.

title: The algebraic structure of the generalized uncertainty principle
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_9309034_The algebraic structure of the generalized uncertainty princ.txt
summary: This paper establishes that the generalized uncertainty principle in quantum gravity emerges from a deformed Heisenberg algebra parametrized by a dimensionful constant κ. The authors show this algebraic structure is uniquely determined by simple physical assumptions and reproduces string theory results to first order in Δp/M_PL, with implications for Planck-scale Lorentz invariance.

title: Lectures on 2D gravity and 2D string theory (TASI 1992)
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_9304011_Lectures on 2D gravity and 2D string theory _TASI 1992_.txt
summary: Comprehensive lectures on two-dimensional quantum gravity and string theory, focusing on c=1 matter coupled to gravity. Covers conformal field theory, Liouville theory, path integral and canonical quantization approaches, matrix models with orthogonal polynomials and free fermion methods, collective field theory, and string scattering amplitudes in 2D spacetime.

title: The international pulsar timing array project: using pulsars as a gravitational wave detector
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\0911.5206_The international pulsar timing array project_ using pulsars.txt
summary: The International Pulsar Timing Array project uses precise timing measurements of millisecond pulsars from global observatories to detect ultra-low frequency gravitational waves (10^-9 to 10^-8 Hz) produced by merging supermassive black hole binaries in galactic centers. The project combines Northern and Southern hemisphere data to search for signatures of these cosmological gravitational wave sources.

title: Holography and Phenomenology
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_0012148_Holography and Phenomenology.txt
summary: This paper explores holographic duality between warped AdS5 geometries with boundary branes and strongly coupled conformal field theories coupled to dynamical gravity. It examines compactifications with 5-dimensional gauge fields and uses the holographic picture to address key phenomenological questions including black hole production, radius stabilization, early universe cosmology, and gauge coupling unification.

title: Classical Equations for Quantum Systems
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\gr-qc_9210010_Classical Equations for Quantum Systems.txt
summary: This paper investigates how classical deterministic laws emerge from quantum mechanics in closed systems through decoherent histories and coarse graining. The authors establish requirements for coarse-grained variables to exhibit quasiclassical behavior with classical equations of motion plus fluctuations, demonstrating that substantial coarse graining beyond uncertainty principle estimates is necessary. They derive phenomenological equations of motion explicitly and establish quantitative connections between decoherence, noise, dissipation, and classical predictability.

title: Indirect Collider Signals for Extra Dimensions
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-ph_9811356_Indirect Collider Signals for Extra Dimensions.txt
summary: This paper examines collider signatures of large extra dimensions and low-scale quantum gravity through indirect effects of Kaluza-Klein graviton exchanges. It analyzes fermion pair production in electron-positron annihilation and Drell-Yan processes at hadron colliders, including novel gluon-initiated contributions. The study derives TeV-scale bounds on the string scale and shows that angular distributions of spin-2 graviton exchanges provide distinctive signatures distinguishing low-scale quantum gravity from other new physics.

title: Physical Aspects of the Space-Time Torsion
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_0103093_Physical Aspects of the Space-Time Torsion.txt
summary: This paper comprehensively reviews quantum aspects of spacetime torsion, examining whether torsion exists and can be detected. It covers classical gravity with torsion, quantum field theory renormalization in torsion backgrounds, particle dynamics and physical effects, observational bounds, and propagating torsion fields. The analysis shows that quantum consistency requires torsion mass far exceeding fermion masses, making experimental detection of propagating torsion highly unlikely.
approaches: quantum field theory in curved spacetime

title: Renormalization group improved black hole spacetimes
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_0002196_Renormalization group improved black hole spacetimes.txt
summary: This paper studies quantum gravitational effects in spherically symmetric black holes by renormalization group improving the Schwarzschild metric using running Newton's constant from effective average action evolution. The resulting quantum spacetime exhibits Reissner-Nordström-like conformal structure with a critical mass M_cr near the Planck scale. The work predicts black hole evaporation terminates at M_cr, leaving a cold soliton-like remnant with AdS₂×S² near-horizon geometry, while the classical singularity is either removed or significantly softened.

title: A Model for High Energy Scattering in Quantum Gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_9906038_A Model for High Energy Scattering in Quantum Gravity.txt
summary: This paper presents a quantum gravity scattering model for high-energy two-body collisions above the Planck scale. At small impact parameters, inelastic scattering is dominated by black hole formation and Hawking decay, while elastic scattering is suppressed. At large impact parameters, graviton exchange dominates. The authors propose that nonlinear classical solutions may describe intermediate regimes and discuss black hole production signatures in low-scale quantum gravity scenarios with extra dimensions.

title: Noncommutative Black Holes, The Final Appeal To Quantum Gravity: A Review

txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\hep-th_0407115_Loop Quantum Gravity and the Cyclic Universe.txt

The analysis shows that LQG-induced changes to the equations of motion in the kinetic-dominated regime cause the scalar field to freeze, allowing the universe to bounce back naturally without reaching the classical singularity present in standard cyclic models.

title: Consistency Check on Volume and Triad Operator Quantisation in Loop Quantum Gravity I

title: Cosmological footprints of loop quantum gravity

title: B-modes and the Nature of Inflation

They identify a critical sound speed threshold (cs ≈ 0.47) marking the boundary of non-trivial inflationary dynamics.

title: Cosmological applications of loop quantum gravity

title: Detailed black hole state counting in loop quantum gravity

title: A generalized Hamiltonian Constraint Operator in Loop Quantum Gravity and its simplest Euclidean Matrix Elements

title: U(N) tools for Loop Quantum Gravity: The Return of the Spinor

title: A New Spin Foam Model for 4d Gravity

title: Spin Foam Models for Quantum Gravity

title: Spin Foam Models
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\gr-qc_9709052_Spin Foam Models.txt

title: A new spinfoam vertex for quantum gravity

title: Group Field Theory: An overview

title: Loop Quantum Cosmology, Boundary Proposals, and Inflation
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\gr-qc_0303072_Loop Quantum Cosmology_ Boundary Proposals_ and Inflation.txt
summary: This paper compares loop quantum cosmology with Wheeler-DeWitt quantum cosmology for closed isotropic models, examining how dynamical initial conditions relate to boundary proposals like the no-boundary and tunneling proposals. It analyzes the emergence of inflation from quantum cosmological frameworks, providing insights into the connection between quantum geometry effects and classical cosmological evolution.

title: Detailed analysis of the predictions of loop quantum cosmology for the primordial power spectra
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1509.05693_Detailed analysis of the predictions of loop quantum cosmolo.txt
summary: This paper provides a comprehensive numerical analysis of primordial power spectra predictions in loop quantum cosmology with post-bounce inflation. The authors map the phenomenologically relevant parameter space, constrain it using observational data, characterize LQC-corrections to observable quantities, and establish a framework for comparing theory with upcoming polarization measurements and computing higher-order observables like non-Gaussianity.

title: Loop Quantum Cosmology: Recent Progress
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\gr-qc_0402053_Loop Quantum Cosmology_ Recent Progress.txt
summary: Loop quantum cosmology applies symmetry reductions of loop quantum gravity to study quantum gravitational effects in cosmological models. The approach eliminates classical singularities, replacing them with quantum bounce scenarios, and provides new inflationary mechanisms for early universe evolution. This overview summarizes recent progress from multiple research groups.

title: Curvature invariants, geodesics and the strength of singularities in Bianchi-I loop quantum cosmology
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1112.6391_Curvature invariants_ geodesics and the strength of singular.txt
summary: This paper investigates how quantum geometry effects in loop quantum cosmology affect spacetime curvature invariants and geodesic extendibility in anisotropic Bianchi-I models. Using effective Hamiltonian methods, the authors demonstrate that quantum geometric effects bound energy density and expansion scalars, preventing strong singularities even when curvature invariants potentially diverge. For perfect fluids with equation of state w > -1, curvature invariants remain bounded and geodesics remain extendible, showing general resolution of singularities in LQC.

title: Genericness of Big Bounce in isotropic loop quantum cosmology
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\gr-qc_0407074_Genericness of Big Bounce in isotropic loop quantum cosmolog.txt
summary: This paper demonstrates that the Big Bounce in isotropic loop quantum cosmology is generic, occurring independently of quantization ambiguities and scalar field details. The bounce volume depends on a single parameter that establishes a minimum length scale, acting as a cutoff for density perturbation computations and affecting their amplitudes.

title: Quantum Gravity from Causal Dynamical Triangulations: A Review
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1905.08669_Quantum Gravity from Causal Dynamical Triangulations_ A Revi.txt
summary: This review surveys Causal Dynamical Triangulations (CDT), a lattice formulation of quantum gravity that derives a quantum gravity theory nonperturbatively from a scaling limit. CDT provides computational access to Planckian spacetime while maintaining manifest diffeomorphism invariance. Recent developments include phase structure analysis, understanding of time and causality roles, renormalization group methods, and new quantum observables in four dimensions.

title: A second-order phase transition in CDT
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1108.3932_A second-order phase transition in CDT.txt
summary: The paper presents evidence for a second-order phase transition in Causal Dynamical Triangulations (CDT), a nonperturbative approach to quantum gravity. This discovery is significant because it provides a mechanism to define a continuum limit similar to conventional lattice field theories, potentially establishing a rigorous path to quantum gravity through lattice regularization.

title: CDT meets Horava-Lifshitz gravity
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1002.3298_CDT meets Horava-Lifshitz gravity.txt
summary: This paper establishes a connection between causal dynamical triangulations (CDT) and Hořava-Lifshitz gravity by comparing their phase diagrams. The authors demonstrate that CDT's phase structure exhibits features characteristic of Lifshitz scaling, suggesting CDT could serve as a nonperturbative framework unifying both anisotropic and isotropic quantum gravity theories through their shared use of global time foliation.

title: Second- and First-Order Phase Transitions in CDT
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1205.1229_Second- and First-Order Phase Transitions in CDT.txt
summary: This paper investigates phase transitions in four-dimensional Causal Dynamical Triangulations (CDT), a discrete approach to quantum gravity. Using Monte Carlo simulations, the authors characterize the boundaries of the de Sitter phase, demonstrating that the A-C transition is first-order while the B-C transition is second-order. The second-order transition may indicate an ultraviolet fixed point relevant to Planck-scale physics.

title: Quantizing Horava-Lifshitz Gravity via Causal Dynamical Triangulations
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1111.6634_Quantizing Horava-Lifshitz Gravity via Causal Dynamical Tria.txt
summary: This paper extends causal dynamical triangulations to quantize (2+1)-dimensional projectable Horava-Lifshitz gravity by incorporating discrete curvature squared terms. Using MCMC simulations on spacetimes with 2-sphere spacelike hypersurfaces, the authors discover multiple macroscopic phases consistent with classical equations of motion, including a phase transition between time-dependent de Sitter-like and time-independent phases, potentially related to Hamiltonian deconfinement.

title: The Semiclassical Limit of Causal Dynamical Triangulations
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1102.3929_The Semiclassical Limit of Causal Dynamical Triangulations.txt
summary: This paper investigates the semiclassical limit of causal dynamical triangulations (CDT), a nonperturbative quantum gravity approach. Through detailed analysis of three-volume data from simulations with a novel four-volume fixing technique, the authors confirm that CDT's macroscopic structure is compatible with de Sitter spacetime, examine short-distance discretization artifacts, and probe higher-order curvature corrections to the effective scale factor action.

title: Spectral dimension as a probe of the ultraviolet continuum regime of causal dynamical triangulations
txt_file: c:\Users\JP\Desktop\qg\papers\1105.5646_Spectral dimension as a probe of the ultraviolet continuum r.txt
summary: This paper investigates the ultraviolet continuum regime of causal dynamical triangulations using spectral dimension as a probe. The authors develop a framework to identify continuum theories reproducing this regime's behavior and demonstrate that Hořava-Lifshitz gravity accurately mimics the spectral dimension flow in 2+1 dimensional CDT across multiple scales, suggesting a deep connection between these quantum gravity approaches.
